perturbation.original_text: In consideration of Big Sky's disclosure to Holdings of the Information, Holdings agrees that it will keep the Information confidential and that the Information will not, without the prior written consent of Big Sky, be disclosed by Holdings or by its officers, directors, employees, agents or representatives (collectively, "Representatives"), in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by Holdings or by its Representatives, other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of Big Sky's disclosure to Holdings of the Data, Holdings agrees that it will keep the Data protected and that the Data will not, without the prior written consent of Big Sky, be disclosed by Holdings or by its officers, directors, employees, agents or representatives (collectively, "Representatives"), in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by Holdings or by its Representatives, other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Data". While similar, these are undefined terms. Later, 'information' is referenced. If Data, under various data protection laws, has specific meanings and restrictions, it makes it unclear if those laws apply and what is being protected. The "Information" referenced later in the document, can cause confusion as to whether or not the prior definition (Data) is still valid. Thus, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of Big Sky's disclosure to Holdings of the Data, Holdings agrees that it will keep the Data protected and that the Data will not, without the prior written consent of Big Sky, be disclosed by Holdings or by its officers, directors, employees, agents or representatives (collectively, "Representatives"), in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by Holdings or by its Representatives, other than in connection with evaluating the Transaction. Moreover, Big Sky agrees to transmit the Information only to such of its Representatives who need to know the Information for the sole purpose of assisting Holdings in evaluating the Transaction and who are informed of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Data'. The explanation states this creates a contradiction because 'Data', under various data protection laws, has specific meanings and restrictions, making it unclear if those laws apply and what is being protected. The contradicted text uses 'Information' suggesting that it is now unclear if 'Data' and 'Information' are the same. This represents misaligned terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, leading to conflicting obligations and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the above restrictions shall not apply to the following: (i) Information that is publicly available at the time of disclosure (through no act of Holdings of any of its Representatives); (ii) Information that is disclosed to Holdings by a third party that did not disclose it in violation of a duty of confidentiality; (iii) Information that was known to Holdings before such Information was provided to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of Big Sky; (iv) disclosures that are required to be made by Holdings or any of its Representatives under legal process by subpoena or other court order or other applicable laws or regulations (provided, however, that Holdings makes reasonable efforts to provide copies of such Information to, or informs Big Sky before disclosure), or that are requested by Big Sky.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the above restrictions shall not apply to the following: (i) Data that is publicly available at the time of disclosure (through no act of Holdings of any of its Representatives); (ii) Information that is disclosed to Holdings by a third party that did not disclose it in violation of a duty of confidentiality; (iii) Intelligence that was known to Holdings before such Information was provided to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of Big Sky; (iv) disclosures that are required to be made by Holdings or any of its Representatives under legal process by subpoena or other court order or other applicable laws or regulations (provided, however, that Holdings makes reasonable efforts to provide copies of such Information to, or informs Big Sky before disclosure), or that are requested by Big Sky.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the first instance of "Information" to "Data" and the third instance of "Information" to "Intelligence" causes a contradiction. The paragraph now references three different terms: Data, Information, and Intelligence. It's unclear what falls under each definition and if each one is different. The definition earlier in the text defined "Information" so that the Data and Intelligence are not included. This creates confusion and makes enforcement difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the above restrictions shall not apply to the following: (i) Data that is publicly available at the time of disclosure (through no act of Holdings of any of its Representatives); (ii) Information that is disclosed to Holdings by a third party that did not disclose it in violation of a duty of confidentiality; (iii) Intelligence that was known to Holdings before such Information was provided to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of Big Sky; (iv) disclosures that are required to be made by Holdings or any of its Representatives under legal process by subpoena or other court order or other applicable laws or regulations (provided, however, that Holdings makes reasonable efforts to provide copies of such Information to, or informs Big Sky before disclosure), or that are requested by Big Sky.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Information' with 'Data' and 'Intelligence' in specific instances while other instances retain 'Information'. Given the Perturbation Explanation indicates that 'Information' was previously defined, the introduction of undefined terms 'Data' and 'Intelligence' creates a contradiction by using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, which could lead to conflicting obligations, thus meeting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Without the prior written consent of Big Sky, neither Holdings nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof. Likewise, without the prior written consent of Holdings, neither Big Sky nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Without the prior written consent of Big Sky, neither Holdings nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Intelligence or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Deal, including the status thereof. Likewise, without the prior written consent of Holdings, neither Big Sky nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Intelligence" in the first sentence and "Transaction" to "Deal" creates ambiguity. The second sentence still refers to "Information" and "Transaction", creating a conflict: Does the restriction apply to Intelligence or just Information? Does it apply to the Deal, the Transaction, or both? This contradiction undermines the purpose of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the prior written consent of Big Sky, neither Holdings nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Intelligence or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Deal, including the status thereof. Likewise, without the prior written consent of Holdings, neither Big Sky nor its Representatives shall disclose to any person the fact that Holdings has received any Information or that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces misaligned terminology by changing "Information" to "Intelligence" and "Transaction" to "Deal" in the first sentence while the second sentence in both the changed text and contradicted text still refers to "Information" and "Transaction." This creates a clear contradiction because it is unclear whether the restriction applies to Intelligence or just Information, and whether it applies to the Deal, the Transaction, or both, undermining the purpose of the NDA as described in the Perturbation Explanation and aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Information, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Information, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered by Holdings and its Representatives to Big Sky.
perturbation.changed_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Material, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Documentation, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered by Holdings and its Representatives to Big Sky.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Material" and "Information" to "Documentation" introduces new undefined terms and a contradiction. Since it is unclear what consitutes 'Material', it creates a loophole. Similarly, 'Documentation' has no clear definition, creating confusion about whether these terms are referring to the same subject. The document is in conflict with itself as to what exactly must be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Big Sky and Holdings agree that, at the conclusion of their review of the Material, or within three business days of the other's request, all copies of the Documentation, in any form whatsoever, will be delivered by Holdings and its Representatives to Big Sky. Neither Big Sky nor any of its Representatives has made or makes any representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'Material' and 'Documentation' which are undefined terms within the contract. The original text used 'Information,' and the contradicted text refers to both 'Material' and 'Documentation', creating ambiguity as to what exactly must be returned. The perturbation explanation states that this creates a loophole and confusion, therefore creating a direct contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition: 'Misaligned terminology also leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'

perturbation.original_text: Holdings agrees that neither Big Sky nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to it or to any of its Representatives resulting from the provision or use of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Holdings agrees that neither Big Sky nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to it or to any of its Representatives resulting from the provision or use of the Data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Data" creates a contradiction and impacts the enforceability of the contract. Because the 'Information' is a defined term, the new use of 'Data' causes confusion whether Data and Information are the same, leading to potential argument that the data is outside of the Liability waiver, rendering a certain piece of the NDA unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Holdings agrees that neither Big Sky nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to it or to any of its Representatives resulting from the provision or use of the Data. Each party acknowledges that it has been informed that the other party is a publicly-traded corporation and that the Information and the Transaction must be secured from any use or dissemination until such time as proper disclosure can be provided to shareholders and the securities markets in order to protect the interests of shareholders of each party and so that the rules and statutes pertaining to information about and trading in publicly held securities can be followed.
perturbation.justification: The original text used the defined term 'Information,' while the changed text replaces it with 'Data.' The contradicted text also uses 'Data' and 'Information', and the perturbation explanation highlights that this creates a contradiction because it introduces uncertainty about whether 'Data' and 'Information' are the same, leading to potential arguments and impacting enforceability. The contradicted text refers to both 'Information' and 'Data', further implying that the change from 'Information' to 'Data' would lead to confusion about the scope of the agreement, given the term 'Information' is defined elsewhere. The introduction of the undefined term 'Data' in a section previously using 'Information', where both terms are used in the Contradicted Text, constitutes misaligned terminology creating an in-text contradiction.
